channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: How to design usage-based pricing
item.description: Usage-based pricing is four decisions in a trenchcoat: what you meter, what unit you charge for, how you structure the rate card, and how you handle commits and overages. I've seen many teams get...

item.title: The Hidden Problem With Learning Through AI
item.description: AI is an incredible learning tool. I said it in my previous article, and I still believe it. But after spending months learning new web frameworks and technologies with AI as my main companion, I...

item.title: Dependency Vulnerability Pattern: Management Status in Small Projects
item.description: When dealing with small and medium-sized projects, dependency vulnerability management is often an overlooked but troublesome issue. At first, everything seems fine; you add your package, your...

item.title: Alphabet’s record-breaking $85B raise for Google’s AI business is a helluva good signal
item.description: If Alphabet's record-breaking, $85 billion stock sale signals investor appetite for AI-related offerings, we can see that investors are ready to chow.

item.title: I had a folder full of dead repos. So I built a graveyard — and a way to raise them.
item.description: This is a submission for the GitHub Finish-Up-A-Thon Challenge Try it now — no login: https://chintanonweb.github.io/lazarus/ · Code: https://github.com/chintanonweb/lazarus 💡 In plain English:...

item.title: 5 Architecture Mistakes I Made as a Full-Stack Developer (And What They Taught Me)
item.description: 5 Architecture Mistakes I Made as a Full-Stack Developer (And What They Taught Me) After more than 9 years building web and mobile applications with React, Node.js, TypeScript, PostgreSQL,...

item.title: I Built an AI-Powered Meeting Platform From Scratch — Here’s How It Actually Works
item.description: A complete breakdown of Hoovik: WebRTC signaling, distributed Node.js with Redis, real-time emotion AI, RAG on meeting transcripts, and a Python transcription pipeline — all wired together. 👉...

item.title: How the State.js Ecosystem Solves the Performance vs. Experience Paradox in Modern E‑Commerce
item.description: Modern premium e‑commerce has a problem nobody talks about enough: Luxury brands want fluid, tactile, high‑end interactions — but the JS runtimes required to achieve them destroy performance....

item.title: Building LifeFast: A Solo Founder's Deep Dive into Fasting App Architecture
item.description: There's a specific kind of frustration that comes from using a health app that feels like it was built by engineers who've never actually used it. The timer shows a number. The number counts down....

item.title: How We Encrypt X Auth Tokens: AES-256-GCM in Practice
item.description: When you build a tool that stores authentication tokens for other people's social media accounts, you have exactly one job before anything else: make sure a database leak doesn't compromise every...

item.title: The MCP Rug Pull - When the Tool You Trusted Yesterday Becomes Malicious Today
item.description: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is having its npm moment. Hundreds of community-built servers expose database access, GitHub APIs, Slack, Notion, your local filesystem. You install one with a...

item.title: Google’s Dreambeans, its weirdest-named AI tool to date, will turn your life into a cartoon
item.description: Dreambeans is a curated list of AI-illustrated "stories" culled from the personal data in your Google account.

item.title: The best Bluetooth trackers for Apple and Android phones
item.description: Some people rarely lose things. Wallets are always exactly where they’re supposed to be, keys never go missing, and remotes never slip between the couch cushions. And then there’s the rest of us —...

item.title: xAI Asks Court to Strip Alleged Grok Deepfake Nudes Victims of Anonymity
item.description: Four people suing Elon Musk's AI firm under pseudonyms due to the risks of being identified may face a difficult choice: Reveal your real names, or drop the lawsuit.

item.title: The Humanoid Robot of the Future Is a 6-Foot-Tall Beefcake With a Chinese Body and an American Brain
item.description: Spencer Huang, Nvidia’s robotics lead, tells WIRED that the new bot combines the best of both worlds.

item.title: The best Qi2 batteries for iPhone and Pixel
item.description: Compact power banks have gotten a lot faster in the past year — and it’s not just their USB-C charging speeds that have received a boost. The newest Qi2.2-certified models can wirelessly charge an...

item.title: As AI gets better, it reveals an empty promise
item.description: This week we've got tandem hands-ons with Google's new Gemini AI agent - Spark - from my colleagues David Pierce and Jay Peters. Their takeaways are similar: It's so effective that it's scary....

item.title: How virtual power plants could provide energy for data centers
item.description: Would you take a payment to ramp down your electricity use? Would it change anything if you were doing so to help power a local data center? Google just signed a new deal to help pay for a virtual...

item.title: Amazon’s search bar will invent AI-generated products you can’t buy
item.description: Amazon's updated search bar will now show you AI-generated images of products as you describe them. For now, the in-app feature only surfaces AI images of clothing and home goods, allowing you to...

item.title: Amazon will show AI product images when you search for some reason
item.description: Amazon will use visual search and AI to show AI-generated product images that match your search queries. The retailer says it will help guide users to products.

item.title: This Is How Trump Finally Signed the AI Executive Order
item.description: After shelving the original executive order last month, Donald Trump finally got on board Monday night.

item.title: PlayStation is getting back to what it’s good at
item.description: PlayStation used its most recent State of Play showcase to make it clear where its focus is. After a series of costly live-service stumbles, it's getting back to focusing on premium,...

item.title: These two founders left Goldman and Meta to build voice AI for markets everyone else overlooked
item.description: The startup's own stack for Africa and Middle East is now handling more than 17,000 calls per day.

item.title: Supernatural isn’t dead after all
item.description: A few months ago, Meta effectively handed Supernatural, a popular VR fitness game on the Meta Quest, a death sentence. As part of overarching VR layoffs, the company announced the game would no...

item.title: Publishers will be able to opt out of AI Search, thanks to new regulation
item.description: U.K. regulators are requiring Google offer a tool allowing website publishers to opt-out of generative AI search features. The option will be tested in the U.K. then rolled out globally.

item.title: Dreame’s L20 Ultra robovac is an unbeatable deal for $280
item.description: The Dreame L20 Ultra isn’t the company’s newest model, but it’s still a great robovac / mop hybrid that offers strong performance while requiring very little day-to-day maintenance thanks to its...

item.title: Microsoft and OpenAI broke up — now they’re ready to fight
item.description: At Microsoft's annual Build conference on Tuesday, the company announced a slew of new or expanded AI initiatives, including a super app, in-house reasoning models, a cybersecurity tool, and...

item.title: Meta’s AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally
item.description: WhatsApp will charge businesses for using its AI agent based on token usage.

item.title: A first look at Microsoft’s Surface Laptop Ultra and Surface Dev Box
item.description: Microsoft has two new Surface devices arriving later this year, both powered by Nvidia's RTX Spark chips. I got a chance to take a closer look at both the Surface Laptop Ultra and Surface RTX...

item.title: One-Click GitHub Dev Attack Lets Attackers Steal Full GitHub OAuth Tokens
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a one-click attack via Microsoft Visual Studio Code (VS Code) that makes it possible to steal a user's GitHub token. "Just by clicking a link, it's...
